violations.text: Cases of bottled water were observed stored on the ground in the outdoor display. New Violation. Correct By: 05/14/2025
Several refrigerator door gaskets throughout the facility were observed not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The cutting boards in the produce prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walls and shelving of the poultry open air case have an accumulation of white debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
The grease trap at the 3-bay sink in the produce prep area is lacking a cover. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The fan guards in several walk-ins have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Ice accumulation was observed around the condensate line in the dairy walk-in. New Violation. Correct By: 05/28/2025
ZServeral ceiling tiles in the produce prep area were falling and/or missing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

An employee was observed washing their hands at the produce 3-bay sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. PIC instructed staff to use hand washing sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink in the meat department was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 200 ppm. PIC had staff dilute concentration during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

Recently cut watermelon was observed held at 59 degrees Fahrenheit in the open-air case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff moved watermelon to walk-in to rapidly chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

Fish was observed held at 40-45 degrees Fahrenheit in the open-air case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Packages were unstacked and placed in single layer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

Working containers of water and dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

The receiving gate to the outside is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Fly tape was observed tucked in ceiling tiles over the produce preparation area. PIC had staff remove the tape during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025

Produce was observed on a gondola stored under a dripping condensate line in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. PIC relocated gondola. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. A staff member removed utensils from the water during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2025
Refrigerator door gaskets on several units were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2025

The cutting boards in the produce area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -proofing cabinet doors -bread slicer -open air case vents. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
The sinks used for washing produce are lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
The grease trap container at the produce 3-bay sink is lacking a cover. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
Debris was observed on mechanical equipment in the trash compactor room.Trash was observed on floor in trash compactor room. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025
The fan guards in the walk-in coolers have an accumulation of dust and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/15/2025

The following were observed in poor repair:-condensate lines in produce walk-in and dairy walk-in freezer-produce walk-in door-floor tiles/covering in sales area-several ceiling tiles missing/falling.The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

Reach-in freezers located in the sales floor area were observed not in good repair. Inspector observed a section of the reach-in freezers with an ambient temperature of 26-36 degrees and multiple foods defrosted/soft to touch. Foods that needed to maintain frozen state and were not frozen were disposed. Service order placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/10/2025
Freezer door gaskets were observed in poor repair on the customer facing units. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2025

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150 -400 ppm. Sanitizer will be added manually until adjusted. Sanitizer logs are to be maintained each time the sink is filled. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the kitchen and deli area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the deli area. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2024
The refrigerator door gaskets observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The floors are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
The food prep sink in the deli area is in poor repair, with material rubberbanded around faucet. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The gasket on the walk in freezer door is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The 3 bay sink is in poor repair, dripping into a bucket when emptying. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
The hand-washing sink in the meat area was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink in the deli area was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Service was called during inspection. All equipment and utensils are to be sanitized before use. Sanitizer logs are to be maintained daily to ensure proper sanitizer concentrations are used. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, numerous utensils in storage bin) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

American Chop Suey, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. American chop suey was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

The doors to the outside, located in the loading docks, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and freezer. Food observe stored on shelves near deli are that are not at least 6 inches above the floor. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

Sheet pans observed stored on the floor between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

Utensils on clean storage rack observed stored in an unclean container. Cleaned equipment and utensils must be stored in a clean, dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the meat area. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the deli area, deli prep area. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The meat band saw cover was not in good repair, (cracked). Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
Air curtains in the produce area were not in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:deli area shelves, including the undersidesdeli display case door tracksdeli caseshelf beneath slicercan opener baseplastic cartsunder-counter plastic binsutensil storage binsrolling racks6-burner stoveoven grease receptaclesice machinemeat area knife rackmeat grinder Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024

The floors (including inside walk in coolers and beneath market shelves) and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris.The walk in freezer has an accumulation of frost and debrris.Light fixtures and ceiling panels in the deli food prep area have an accumulation of food residue, dust and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The floors are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2025
The food prep sink in the deli area is in poor repair, with material rubberbanded around faucet. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The lights beneath the hood were nonfunctional during inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The outside loading dock areas have an accumulation of litter and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/08/2024
The gasket on the walk in freezer door is in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the deli prep and meat areas. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
